SINE Tribes — 99% copy clusters

SINEtribes-style clustering of the full Step1 extracted hit bank (genome.clean_step1/extracted.fasta) using vsearch --cluster_fast --id 0.99 --strand plus. Tribes require at least 5 copies. The first 10 tribes use up to 500 copies sampled with awk/shuf; each MSA keeps the MAFFT-aligned SINE body and adds strand-aware unaligned flanks (50 bp left, 70 bp right) from genome.clean.fa. Full TSV Showing the largest 30 tribes here; the TSV contains all 320 tribes.

Overview

This run searched GCF_000260355.1_ConCri1.0_genomic.fna with 7 subfamily consensuses from tal.bnk. After merging overlapping hits across queries, 463,717 candidate SINE copies entered step2. Of those, 383,477 (82.7%) were unanimously called by all 10 sub-samples, 380,406 (82.0%) passed the bitscore threshold and received a final subfamily label, and 83,311 (18.0%) remained unassigned.

Pipeline counts

Total sequences (step1 merged hits entering step2)463,717
└ Unanimous (10/10 sub-sample votes)383,477 (82.7%)
✓ Assigned (passed bitscore threshold)380,406 (82.0%)
✗ Unassigned83,311 (18.0%)

Assigned + Unassigned ≈ Total. Unanimous is a strict subset; assigned includes unanimous + soft-assigned copies that passed the bitscore threshold.

Subfamily composition

Source: summary.by_subfam.tsv. Highlighted columns (bold): total copies, similarity mean / median, conflict rate. Hover a column header for its full description.

Column legend
  • Subfamily — Subfamily name.
  • Firm — Copies whose primary assignment uses 10/10 votes and passed the threshold (high-confidence).
  • Soft — Copies promoted via soft-rules (ssearch36 tie-breaking on otherwise-unassigned).
  • Total copies — firm_assigned + soft_assigned.
  • Leaks — Copies of OTHER subfamilies that, in step3 sanity checks, leaked into this subfamily's intervals.
  • Conflicts — Genomic intervals where this subfamily is the winning name but at least one other subfamily also had hits (CONFLICT-flagged).
  • Firm % — firm_assigned as % of all firm assignments.
  • Total % — total_assigned as % of all assignments.
  • Leak % — leak_n as % of total_assigned.
  • Conflict % — conf_alt_n as % of total_assigned.
  • Sim mean — Mean per-copy bitscore / consensus self-bits.
  • Sim median — Median per-copy bitscore / consensus self-bits.

Divergence from consensus — per copy

Metric: bitscore-based divergence = 100 − (copy bitscore / consensus self-bitscore × 100 %). This is a proxy for sequence divergence, not a direct nucleotide count. Values are clamped to 0 (local alignment can occasionally score a copy above the self-bitscore, which would otherwise appear as negative divergence). The histogram uses all assigned copies with similarity scores, grouped into 0.5 percentage-point divergence bins; bar height is copy count. Colors show subfamilies; click legend entries to toggle.

Distributions per subfamily (violin)

Same data as above shown as symmetric violin plots. Width encodes density; inner box = IQR; dashed line = mean.

Source: sim_scores.tsv joined with assignment_full.tsv.

PCA — mutation landscape

Each point is one assigned SINE copy, coloured by subfamily. Axes are PC1 / PC2 of a binary mutation matrix: each alignment column where the copy nucleotide differs from its assigned-subfamily consensus is encoded as 1, matches as 0. Only variable columns (mutation rate 3–97 %) are used. Copies with distinct mutation patterns cluster together — subfamily clouds are expected when subfamilies have different characteristic mutations.

Method: mafft --add --keeplength adds sampled copies into the consensus alignment (up to 200 per subfamily). Binary mutation matrix → SVD. η²(subfamily→PC1) = fraction of PC1 variance explained by subfamily label (0 = no separation, 1 = perfect).

Per-subfamily diagnostic plots (step4)

7 subfamilies. Each block: divergence histogram (top) + nucleotide frequency stacked bar (bottom). Embedded as base64 PNGs.

Step2 — assignment stats per subfamily

Output of step2_asSINEment.sh (assignment_stats.tsv). Assigned = copies that passed the bitscore threshold.

Column legend
  • Subfamily — Subfamily name from the consensus FASTA.
  • Assigned — Copies that passed all assignment criteria (unanimous 10/10 vote AND bitscore ≥ threshold).
  • TopN_Bitscore — Bitscore of the N-th best per-subfamily hit (N = min(10, count)); reference for the threshold.
  • Threshold — Cutoff applied: 0.45 × TopN_Bitscore × 100. Copies below this are unassigned.
SubfamilyAssignedTopN_BitscoreThreshold

Bitscore thresholds vs consensus self-bits

Threshold is stored as integer bits×100; real self-bits = ssearch36 score of the consensus vs itself. Threshold/Self ≈ minimum fractional similarity to pass.

Column legend
  • Subfamily — Subfamily name.
  • Threshold (x100) — Bitscore cutoff used by step2 (stored as integer = bits × 100).
  • RealSelfBits — ssearch36 bitscore of the consensus aligned against itself (raw bitscore).
  • Threshold/Self — Threshold (in bits, divided back by 100) / RealSelfBits. Roughly the minimum fractional similarity to consensus a copy must reach.
SubfamilyThreshold (x100)RealSelfBitsThreshold/Self

Quality flags per subfamily

Merged genomic intervals grouped by sanity flag from step3_postprocess.sh. High %CONFLICT suggests ambiguous subfamily boundaries at those loci.

Column legend
  • Subfamily — Subfamily name (winner of the merged interval).
  • Total — Number of merged genomic intervals labelled with this subfamily.
  • OK — Intervals with no conflict and no leak flag.
  • LEAK — Intervals where this subfamily 'leaked' a hit into an interval owned by a different subfamily.
  • CONFLICT — Intervals where multiple subfamilies had hits in the same merged region.
  • %CONFLICT — 100 × CONFLICT / Total.
SubfamilyTotalOKLEAKCONFLICT%CONFLICT
